title: Git on Android — Version Control Guide | Acode
description: Use Git on your Android phone with Acode. Clone repos, commit changes, push to GitHub, manage branches — full Git workflow via Acode's Alpine Linux terminal and Git plugins.
heroHeadline: Git on Android — Full Version Control on Your Phone
heroSubheadline: Acode gives you a complete Git workflow on Android. Install Git in the Alpine Linux terminal, use CLI commands or GUI plugins, and manage your repositories — all from your phone.
problemHeadline: Git on Android Has Always Been Limited
problemPoints: Git is essential for every developer — but Git GUIs are desktop tools. On Android, you're limited to basic Git clients that can clone repos but can't provide a full development workflow.
You can install Git in Termux, but then you need to switch to a code editor to actually work with the files. Edit in one app, commit in another, push in the first again — it's a fragmented mess.
What developers need is Git integrated into their IDE — just like VS Code's Git panel. Clone, edit, stage, commit, and push without leaving the development environment.
solutionPoints: Install Git in Acode's terminal: apk add git. Configure your identity: git config --global user.name 'Your Name' and git config --global user.email 'email@example.com'. That's it.
Clone repositories, edit files in Acode's editor with syntax highlighting, stage changes in the terminal, commit, and push — all in one app. No switching between Termux and an editor.
Acode also has Git GUI plugins available in the Plugin Store. Get visual diffs, branch management, and commit history — similar to VS Code's Git panel.
Use Git alongside your other development tools — Node.js, Python, SSH, AI agents — all in the same Alpine Linux environment. This is how Git was meant to be used: in an IDE, not isolation.

setupSteps.title: Install Acode & Terminal
setupSteps.description: Download Acode and set up the Alpine Linux terminal.

setupSteps.title: Configure Git
setupSteps.description: Run: git config --global user.name 'Your Name' and git config --global user.email 'email@example.com'

setupSteps.title: Clone a Repository
setupSteps.description: Run: git clone https://github.com/user/repo.git. The repo downloads to your phone.

setupSteps.title: Open in Editor
setupSteps.description: Run: acode repo-name to open the cloned project in Acode's editor.

setupSteps.title: Make Changes & Commit
setupSteps.description: Edit files, then in terminal: git add . && git commit -m 'My changes' && git push

features.title: Full Git CLI
features.description: Every Git command works: clone, pull, push, branch, merge, rebase, stash, log, diff. Git runs natively in Alpine Linux.

features.title: GUI Git Plugins
features.description: Install Git GUI plugins from the Plugin Store for visual diffs, branch management, and commit staging.

features.title: Editor + Git Integration
features.description: Edit files in the code editor, commit from the terminal. Use acode . to open your Git repo in the editor instantly.

features.title: GitHub/GitLab Integration
features.description: Push to GitHub, GitLab, or any Git host. Use SSH keys stored in Acode's terminal for secure authentication.

faqs.question: Is Git on Android as capable as desktop Git?
faqs.answer: Yes. It's the exact same Git binary running in Alpine Linux. All Git commands, including advanced operations like rebase, cherry-pick, and bisect, work exactly as they do on desktop.

faqs.question: Can I use GitHub authentication on Acode?
faqs.answer: Yes. Set up SSH keys in Acode's terminal or use GitHub CLI (gh). You can also use personal access tokens for HTTPS authentication.
